title: Student Rights and Responsibilities: Student Expression
description: Students have the right to express themselves freely at school, but that right comes with boundaries designed to keep the learning environment safe and respectful. The policy explains what kinds of speech and expression are protected, what types are not permitted, and how the district handles student publications, protests, and other forms of communication. Parents and students can use this policy to understand where the line is drawn between personal expression and conduct that disrupts school activities or infringes on the rights of others.
